skill.name: using-superpowers
skill.description: Establishes a workflow for discovering, reading, announcing, and following relevant skills before starting tasks.
skill.summary: Guides assistants to use relevant skills and checklist tracking consistently.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3, 47, and 62, plus system reconnaissance at line 54. These are false positives from prose in a Markdown workflow guide; no executable code, network access, filesystem access, environment access, or command execution was found.

content.value_statement: AI assistants often skip useful task workflows when instructions seem simple. This skill makes skill discovery, announcement, and checklist tracking explicit.
actual_capabilities: Requires checking for relevant skills before starting a task.
Requires reading the current skill instructions before use.
Requires announcing which skill is being used and why.
Requires TodoWrite tracking for every checklist item in a skill.
Explains when to follow rigid workflows exactly.
Clarifies that user goals do not automatically replace process steps.
limitations: Does not perform domain work by itself.
Depends on available skills in the active assistant environment.
May add process overhead for very small tasks.
Does not include scripts, tests, or automated enforcement.

use_cases.title: Standardize Assistant Task Starts
use_cases.description: Ensure every new task begins with skill discovery, reading, and explicit usage announcement.

use_cases.title: Improve Checklist Compliance
use_cases.description: Make assistants turn skill checklists into tracked todos so required steps are not skipped.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Usage Habits
use_cases.description: Provide a simple onboarding workflow for agents that must work across multiple specialized skills.

prompt_templates.title: Start With Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether any available skills apply to this request. Read the relevant skill and tell me how you will use it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for starting a task with skill discovery.

prompt_templates.title: Track Checklist Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the relevant skill for this task. If it includes a checklist, create a tracked todo for each checklist item.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for tasks that require checklist discipline.

prompt_templates.title: Review Process Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current assistant workflow against using-superpowers. Identify missed skill checks, missing announcements, or skipped checklist tracking.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for auditing an assistant process.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply using-superpowers to a multi-step project. Define how skill discovery, skill reading, checklist tracking, implementation, and verification should work.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for planning complex work across multiple skills.

output_examples.input: Start a coding task that may need a testing workflow.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks available skills, reads the relevant testing skill, announces its use, and creates todos for each required checklist item.

output_examples.input: Review whether a previous task followed skill discipline.
output: Skill discovery was performed before implementation.
The chosen skill was announced before use.
Verification steps were completed before handoff.

output_examples.input: Plan a complex project using multiple skills.
output_examples.output: The assistant maps each project phase to relevant skills, reads each skill before use, and keeps separate todos for required workflow steps.

best_practices: Read the current version of each relevant skill before using it.
Announce skill usage before applying its workflow.
Create one tracked todo for each checklist item.
anti_patterns: Assuming a remembered skill is still current.
Skipping skill discovery because a task seems small.
Combining multiple checklist items into one todo.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It defines a workflow for finding, reading, announcing, and following relevant skills before task work begins.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown instruction file and does not include executable code.

faq.question: Why does it mention TodoWrite?
faq.answer: It asks assistants to track checklist items explicitly so required process steps are visible.

faq.question: Can it replace domain-specific skills?
faq.answer: No. It helps select and use other skills, but it does not provide domain-specific implementation guidance.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is written for assistants that can read skills and track task steps.

faq.question: What should users watch for?
faq.answer: Users should ensure the assistant applies this process without letting it delay urgent or clearly bounded work.
